problem_59.natural_language: Eigenvectors are preserved by polynomial functional calculus.
problem_59.lean:
theorem aeval_apply_of_eigenvector
    {K V : Type*} [Field K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]
    (f : Module.End K V) (v : V) (mu : K) (p : Polynomial K)
    (hv : f v = mu • v) :
    (Polynomial.aeval f p) v = (Polynomial.eval mu p) • v
problem_59.natural_language_proof:
Proof: first note that `f^n v = mu^n • v` for all `n`, proved by induction using `hv`. Thus for a
monomial we have `(aeval f (X^n)) v = f^n v = mu^n • v`, which matches `eval mu (X^n) • v`. By
linearity, the same holds for any polynomial `p`, giving
`(Polynomial.aeval f p) v = (Polynomial.eval mu p) • v`.
